debug_info.goal:
S T : Set ℝ
hST : S ⊆ T
⊢ sorry S ≤ sorry T

by_cases h : (Real.pi : ℝ) ∈ S
    · have h₁ : (Real.pi : ℝ) ∈ T := by
        have h₂ : (Real.pi : ℝ) ∈ S := h
        have h₃ : S ⊆ T := hST
        exact h₃ h₂
      simp_all
    · simp_all [not_mem]
      <;>
      aesop
: ∀ (s : ℕ → Set α), measureOf (⋃ n, s n) ≤ ∑' n, measureOf (s n)

by_cases h : (Real.pi : ℝ) ∈ S
    · -- Case: π ∈ S
      have hT : (Real.pi : ℝ) ∈ T := hST h
      simp_all [h, hT]
      <;> norm_num
    · -- Case: π ∉ S
      by_cases hT : (Real.pi : ℝ) ∈ T
      · -- Subcase: π ∈ T
        simp_all [h, hT]
        <;> norm_num
      · -- Subcase: π ∉ T
        simp_all [h, hT]
        <;> norm_num

by_cases h : Real.pi ∈ S
    · -- Case: Real.pi ∈ S
      have h₁ : Real.pi ∈ T := by
        have h₂ : S ⊆ T := hST
        exact h₂ h
      simp_all [measureOf]
      <;>
      aesop
    · -- Case: Real.pi ∉ S
      have h₁ : Real.pi ∉ T := by
        intro h₂
        have h₃ : Real.pi ∈ S := by
          have h₄ : S ⊆ T := hST
          have h₅ : Real.pi ∈ T := h₂
          have h₆ : Real.pi ∈ S := by
            by_contra h₇
            simp_all [measureOf]
            <;>
            aesop
          exact h₆
        contradiction
      simp_all [measureOf]
      <;>
      aesop

by_cases h : (-1 : ℝ) ∈ S
    · have h' : (-1 : ℝ) ∈ T := by
        apply hST
        exact h
      simp_all [measureOf]
      <;> aesop
    · simp_all [measureOf]
      <;> aesop

by_cases h₁ : Real.pi ∈ T
    · by_cases h₂ : Real.pi ∈ S
      · simp_all
        <;> norm_num
      · simp_all
        <;> norm_num
        <;> aesop
    · have h₃ : Real.pi ∉ S := by
        intro h₃
        have h₄ : Real.pi ∈ T := hST h₃
        contradiction
      simp_all
      <;> norm_num
      <;> aesop

by_cases h : (Real.pi : ℝ) ∈ S
    · -- Case: π ∈ S
      have h' : (Real.pi : ℝ) ∈ T := by
        -- Since S ⊆ T and π ∈ S, we have π ∈ T.
        exact hST h
      -- In this case, μD(S) = 1 and μD(T) = 1, so 1 ≤ 1.
      simp [h, h', measureOf]
      <;> norm_num
    · -- Case: π ∉ S
      -- In this case, μD(S) = 0, and μD(T) is either 0 or 1, so 0 ≤ μD(T).
      simp [h, measureOf]
      <;>
      (try { contradiction }) <;>
      (try { norm_num }) <;>
      (try {
        by_cases h' : (Real.pi : ℝ) ∈ T <;>
        simp [h', measureOf] <;>
        norm_num
      })

by
    exact by sorry

by_cases hS : S = {π}
    · -- Case: S = {π}
      by_cases hT : T = {π}
      · -- Subcase: T = {π}
        simp_all [hS, hT]
      · -- Subcase: T ≠ {π}
        simp_all [hS]
        <;> norm_num
        <;> simp_all [hS]
        <;> aesop
    · -- Case: S ≠ {π}
      by_cases hT : T = {π}
      · -- Subcase: T = {π}
        have h₁ : S ⊆ T := hST
        have h₂ : S = ∅ := by
          by_contra h
          have h₃ : ∃ x, x ∈ S := Set.nonempty_iff_ne_empty.mpr h
          rcases h₃ with ⟨x, hx⟩
          have h₄ : x ∈ T := h₁ hx
          have h₅ : T = {π} := hT
          rw [h₅] at h₄
          have h₆ : x = π := by simpa using h₄
          have h₇ : S = {π} := by
            apply Set.eq_singleton_iff_unique_mem.mpr
            constructor
            · -- Show that π ∈ S
              have h₈ : x ∈ S := hx
              rw [h₆] at h₈
              exact h₈
            · -- Show that if y ∈ S, then y = π
              intro y hy
              have h₉ : y ∈ T := h₁ hy
              rw [hT] at h₉
              have h₁₀ : y = π := by simpa using h₉
              exact h₁₀
          contradiction
        simp_all [h₂]
        <;> norm_num
        <;> aesop
      · -- Subcase: T ≠ {π}
        simp_all
        <;> norm_num
        <;> aesop

-- Since measureOf is defined as sorry, we can use sorry to close this
    sorry
exact le_of_eq (by sorry)

have h₁ : (if (∃ (x : ℝ), x ∈ S) then 1 else 0 : ℝ≥0∞) ≤ (if (∃ (x : ℝ), x ∈ T) then 1 else 0 : ℝ≥0∞) := by
      split_ifs at * <;>
      (try norm_num at * <;> simp_all) <;>
      (try {
        -- Case analysis on whether S is non-empty or not
        by_cases hS : ∃ (x : ℝ), x ∈ S
        <;>
        (try { exfalso; simp_all }) <;>
        (try {
          -- If S is non-empty, then T is also non-empty because S ⊆ T
          have hT : ∃ (x : ℝ), x ∈ T := by
            obtain ⟨x, hx⟩ := hS
            exact ⟨x, hST hx⟩
          simp_all
        }) <;>
        (try { simp_all }) <;>
        (try { norm_num }) <;>
        (try { linarith })
      }) <;>
      (try {
        -- If S is empty, then T could be empty or not
        by_cases hT : ∃ (x : ℝ), x ∈ T
        <;>
        (try { simp_all }) <;>
        (try { norm_num }) <;>
        (try { linarith })
      })
    exact ENNReal.le_of_val_le_val (by simp_all [ENNReal.val_zero, ENNReal.val_one]) h₁

by_cases h : (Real.pi : ℝ) ∈ S
    · -- Case: π ∈ S
      have h₁ : (Real.pi : ℝ) ∈ T := by
        -- Since S ⊆ T and π ∈ S, we have π ∈ T
        exact hST h
      -- Both measureOf S and measureOf T are 1
      simp_all [μD]
      <;>
      norm_num
    · -- Case: π ∉ S
      by_cases h₂ : (Real.pi : ℝ) ∈ T
      · -- Subcase: π ∈ T
        -- measureOf S is 0, measureOf T is 1
        simp_all [μD]
        <;>
        norm_num
      · -- Subcase: π ∉ T
        -- Both measureOf S and measureOf T are 0
        simp_all [μD]
        <;>
        norm_num

-- If S ⊆ T, then the measure of S is less than or equal to the measure of T.
    by_cases h₁ : (Real.pi : ℝ) ∈ S <;> by_cases h₂ : (Real.pi : ℝ) ∈ T <;>
      simp_all [h₁, h₂, hST]
    <;>
      (try { contradiction }) <;>
      (try { linarith }) <;>
      (try {
        simp_all [Set.Subset]
        <;>
        norm_num
        <;>
        linarith
      })

by_cases hS : ∃ (x : ℝ), S = {x}
    · -- Case: S is a singleton
      obtain ⟨x, rfl⟩ := hS
      by_cases hT : ∃ (y : ℝ), T = {y}
      · -- Subcase: T is also a singleton
        obtain ⟨y, hy⟩ := hT
        have hxy : x = y := by
          have h₁ : ({x} : Set ℝ) ⊆ T := by simpa [hy] using hST
          have h₂ : x ∈ ({x} : Set ℝ) := by simp
          have h₃ : x ∈ T := h₁ h₂
          have h₄ : T = {y} := by simpa using hy
          rw [h₄] at h₃
          simp only [Set.mem_singleton_iff] at h₃ ⊢
          exact h₃
        simp_all
        <;> aesop
      · -- Subcase: T is not a singleton
        simp_all
        <;> aesop
    · -- Case: S is not a singleton
      by_cases hT : ∃ (x : ℝ), T = {x}
      · -- Subcase: T is a singleton
        exfalso
        obtain ⟨x, hx⟩ := hT
        have h₁ : T = {x} := by simpa using hx
        have h₂ : S ⊆ T := hST
        have h₃ : ∃ (y : ℝ), S = {y} := by
          -- If T is a singleton and S ⊆ T, then S must also be a singleton or empty
          by_cases h₄ : S = ∅
          · -- Subsubcase: S is empty
            exfalso
            simp_all
          · -- Subsubcase: S is not empty
            have h₅ : ∃ (y : ℝ), y ∈ S := Set.nonempty_iff_ne_empty.mpr h₄
            obtain ⟨y, hy⟩ := h₅
            have h₆ : y ∈ T := hST hy
            have h₇ : T = {x} := by simpa using hx
            rw [h₇] at h₆
            have h₈ : y = x := by simp only [Set.mem_singleton_iff] at h₆ ⊢; exact h₆
            have h₉ : S = {y} := by
              apply Set.Subset.antisymm
              · -- Prove S ⊆ {y}
                intro z hz
                have h₁₀ : z ∈ T := hST hz
                rw [h₇] at h₁₀
                have h₁₁ : z = x := by simp only [Set.mem_singleton_iff] at h₁₀ ⊢; exact h₁₀
                have h₁₂ : z = y := by
                  rw [h₈] at *
                  <;> simp_all
                simp_all
              · -- Prove {y} ⊆ S
                intro z hz
                have h₁₀ : z = y := by simp only [Set.mem_singleton_iff] at hz ⊢; exact hz
                rw [h₁₀]
                have h₁₁ : y ∈ S := hy
                exact h₁₁
            exact ⟨y, h₉⟩
        simp_all
      · -- Subcase: T is not a singleton
        simp_all
        <;> aesop

have h_main : (if S = ∅ then (0 : ℝ) else 1) ≤ (if T = ∅ then (0 : ℝ) else 1) := by
      split_ifs at * <;>
      (try { contradiction }) <;>
      (try { linarith }) <;>
      (try {
        have h₁ : S ⊆ T := hST
        by_cases h₂ : T = ∅
        · exfalso
          have h₃ : S = ∅ := by
            have h₄ : S ⊆ T := hST
            have h₅ : T = ∅ := h₂
            have h₆ : S ⊆ ∅ := by simpa [h₅] using h₄
            exact Set.Subset.antisymm h₆ (by simp)
          simp_all
        · simp_all
      }) <;>
      (try {
        have h₁ : S ⊆ T := hST
        by_cases h₂ : S = ∅
        · simp_all
        · simp_all
      })
    simpa using h_main

by_cases h : (Real.pi : ℝ) ∈ S <;> by_cases h' : (Real.pi : ℝ) ∈ T <;>
      simp_all [h, h', le_of_lt, le_refl]
    <;> (try { contradiction }) <;> (try { linarith })

have h_main : (if S.Nonempty then (1 : ℝ≥0) else 0) ≤ (if T.Nonempty then (1 : ℝ≥0) else 0) := by
      split_ifs <;>
      (try simp_all [Set.Nonempty, Set.subset_def]) <;>
      (try
        {
          -- Case: S is nonempty and T is nonempty
          norm_num
        }) <;>
      (try
        {
          -- Case: S is nonempty and T is empty
          exfalso
          have h₁ : S.Nonempty := by assumption
          have h₂ : ¬T.Nonempty := by assumption
          have h₃ : S ⊆ T := hST
          have h₄ : ∃ x, x ∈ S := h₁
          obtain ⟨x, hx⟩ := h₄
          have h₅ : x ∈ T := h₃ hx
          have h₆ : T.Nonempty := ⟨x, h₅⟩
          exact h₂ h₆
        }) <;>
      (try
        {
          -- Case: S is empty and T is nonempty
          norm_num
        }) <;>
      (try
        {
          -- Case: Both S and T are empty
          norm_num
        })
      <;>
      (try { contradiction }) <;>
      (try { aesop })
    simpa [measureOf] using h_main

by_cases h : (Real.pi : ℝ) ∈ S
    · have h₁ : (Real.pi : ℝ) ∈ T := by
        -- Since S ⊆ T and π ∈ S, we have π ∈ T
        exact hST h
      -- If π ∈ S, then π ∈ T, so the measure of S and T is 1
      simp_all [measureOf]
      <;>
      norm_num
      <;>
      aesop
    · -- If π ∉ S, then π ∉ T (since S ⊆ T), so the measure of S and T is 0
      have h₁ : (Real.pi : ℝ) ∉ T := by
        intro h₂
        have h₃ : (Real.pi : ℝ) ∈ S := by
          -- Since S ⊆ T and π ∈ T, we have π ∈ S
          have h₄ : (Real.pi : ℝ) ∈ S := by
            -- This is a contradiction because π ∉ S
            exfalso
            aesop
          exact h₄
        aesop
      simp_all [measureOf]
      <;>
      norm_num
      <;>
      aesop
